factors.summary: The company's business is exposed to risks from government budget uncertainties and potential cuts in defense spending.

evidence.text: The U.S. Government generally has the ability to terminate contracts, in whole or in part, with little or no prior notice, for convenience or for default based on performance. In the event of termination of a contract for the U.S. Government's convenience, a contractor is normally able to recover costs already incurred on the contract and receive profit on those costs up to the amount authorized under the contract, but not the anticipated profit that would have been earned had the contract been completed.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces significant risks from government budget uncertainties and potential defense spending cuts.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable, with most collective bargaining agreements recently renewed and good relationships maintained with represented workers.

evidence.text: While we believe we maintain good relationships with our represented workers, it is possible we may experience difficulties renegotiating expiring collective bargaining agreements.
evidence.subsection: Our business could suffer if we are unsuccessful in negotiating new collective bargaining agreements.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While labor relations are stable with recent renewals, there is a possibility of difficulties in renegotiating agreements.

factors.summary: The company is likely facing challenges in talent retention, particularly in technical fields, as the market demand for skilled workers is high.

evidence.text: Approximately 50% of our employees are covered by a total of nine collective bargaining agreements and two DoE site stabilization agreements.
evidence.subsection: Our business could suffer if we are unsuccessful in negotiating new collective bargaining agreements.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is likely facing challenges in retaining talent, especially in technical fields, due to high market demand.

factors.summary: Demand appears to be stable with mixed trends across segments.

evidence.text: Service revenues in 2017 increased $431 million, or 30%, from 2016. Service revenues at our Ingalls segment increased $41 million in 2017, as a result of higher volumes in surface combatants services. Service revenues at our Newport News segment increased $57 million in 2017, primarily as a result of higher volumes in naval nuclear support services and submarines services, partially offset by lower volumes in aircraft carriers services. Service revenues at our Technical Solutions segment increased $333 million in 2017, primarily as a result of higher volumes in integrated missions solutions services following the December 2016 acquisition of Camber and higher volumes in fleet support and oil and gas services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is stable with growth in service revenues across segments, indicating a positive trend.

factors.summary: The Technical Solutions segment is experiencing the fastest growth, driven by higher volumes in services and products.

evidence.text: Technical Solutions product sales decreased $68 million in 2017, primarily as a result of higher volumes in 2016 from the resolution of outstanding contract changes on a nuclear and environmental commercial contract. Service revenues at our Technical Solutions segment increased $333 million in 2017, primarily as a result of higher volumes in integrated missions solutions services following the December 2016 acquisition of Camber and higher volumes in fleet support and oil and gas services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The Technical Solutions segment is growing rapidly due to higher service volumes and product changes.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, particularly in general and administrative expenses.

evidence.text: General and administrative expenses in 2017 decreased $53 million, or 9%, compared to 2016. This decrease was primarily driven by favorable changes in the FAS/CAS Adjustment and lower current state tax expense.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs have successfully delivered measurable savings in general and administrative expenses.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in the Technical Solutions segment, due to higher service volumes and contract changes.

evidence.text: Service revenues at our Technical Solutions segment increased $333 million in 2017, primarily as a result of higher volumes in integrated missions solutions services following the December 2016 acquisition of Camber and higher volumes in fleet support and oil and gas services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving, particularly in the Technical Solutions segment, driven by higher service volumes and contract changes.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to margin compression and cost changes.

evidence.text: Segment operating income in 2017 was $688 million, compared to $715 million in 2016. The decrease was primarily due to lower risk retirement on the delivered USS John P. Murtha (LPD 26), lower volume and risk retirement in the Virginia class (SSN 774) submarine program and Arleigh Burke class (DDG 51) destroyers, favorable changes in overhead costs in 2016, the receipt in 2016 of a local government incentive grant, and the establishment of an allowance for accounts receivable on a nuclear and environmental commercial contract in 2017, partially offset by higher risk retirement on Tripoli (LHA 7) and Portland (LPD 27), the resolution of outstanding contract changes on the inactivation of the decommissioned Enterprise (CVN 65) and the RCOH of the redelivered USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN 72), and improved performance in oil and gas services.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management appears cautious due to margin compression and cost changes.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes are impacting the company through changes in tax laws and defense budgets.

evidence.text: In December 2017, the U.S. enacted the Tax Act, which made significant changes to U.S. federal income tax law, including a reduction of the statutory federal corporate income tax rate from 35.0% to 21.0% and changes or limitations to certain deductions. As a result of the corporate income tax reduction effective January 1, 2018, we revalued our net deferred tax assets as of December 31, 2017. This reduced our net deferred tax assets by $56 million, which was recorded as additional income tax expense for the year ended December 31, 2017. In future periods, we expect that Tax Act changes will positively impact our after-tax earnings primarily due to the lower corporate income tax rate.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive impact on after-tax earnings due to lower corporate income tax rate.

factors.summary: The company faces significant risks related to litigation, government audits, and investigations, which could potentially lead to financial penalties and reputational damage. While the company does not currently expect a material financial or operational impact from these matters, the risks associated with legal proceedings and regulatory actions remain notable.

evidence.text: We are subject to a range of legal proceedings before various courts and administrative agencies and are periodically subject to government examinations, inquiries, and investigations that arise in the ordinary course of business. Estimating liabilities and costs associated with these matters requires judgment and assessment based upon professional knowledge and the experience of management and our internal and external legal counsel.

If an audit uncovers improper or illegal activities, we may be subject to administrative, civil, or criminal proceedings, which could result in fines, penalties, repayments, or compensatory, treble, or other damages.
evidence.subsection: As a U.S. Government contractor, we are heavily regulated and could be adversely affected by changes in regulations or negative findings from a U.S. Government audit or investigation.

sentiment.label: negative

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a deceleration in organic revenue growth, particularly in the Newport News segment, which saw a 1% decrease in product sales. Additionally, organic growth is constrained by the uncertain defense budget and potential reductions in funding for certain programs, which may lead to fewer contract awards and lower revenues.

evidence.text: Technical Solutions revenues, including intersegment sales, for the year ended December 31, 2017 , increased $261 million , or 38% , compared to 2016 , primarily due to higher volume in integrated missions solutions services following the December 2016 acquisition of Camber and higher volumes in fleet support and oil and gas services, partially offset by lower nuclear and environmental volumes due to the resolution in 2016 of outstanding contract changes on a nuclear and environmental commercial contract.

Product sales in 2017 decreased $58 million, or 1%, from 2016. Newport News product sales decreased $20 million in 2017, primarily as a result of lower volumes in aircraft carriers. Technical Solutions product sales decreased $68 million in 2017, primarily as a result of the resolution of outstanding contract changes on a nuclear and environmental commercial contract in 2016.

The budget environment... may result in fewer contract awards and lower revenues, profits, and cash flows from our U.S. Government contracts. Congress and the new Administration continue to discuss various options to address sequestration in future budget planning, but we cannot predict the outcome of these efforts.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is experiencing a deceleration in organic revenue growth, particularly in the Newport News segment, which is a negative sentiment.

factors.summary: The U.S. defense budget is facing significant pressures, including sequestration and other budget priorities, which could lead to reduced funding for certain programs. However, government defense budgets are also stable, with spending directed toward core program areas, and there are indications that defense spending is growing, particularly in areas aligned with the company's core programs.

evidence.text: The BCA also provided that the defense budget would face 'sequestration' cuts of up to an additional $500 billion during that same period, to the extent that discretionary spending limits are exceeded, and $500 billion for non-defense discretionary spending, including the U.S. Coast Guard. Sequestration remains in effect for fiscal years 2020 and 2021. Long-term uncertainty remains with respect to overall levels of defense spending across the future years defense plan, and it is likely that U.S. Government discretionary spending levels will continue to be subject to significant pressure.

The Bipartisan Budget Act of 2018 (the 'BBA 2018') provided sequestration relief for fiscal years 2018 and 2019. Sequestration remains in effect, however, for fiscal years 2020 and 2021. Long-term uncertainty is continuing with respect to overall levels of defense spending across the future years defense plan, and it is likely that U.S. Government discretionary spending levels will continue to be subject to significant pressure.
U.S. Government orders comprised substantially all of the backlog as of December 31, 2017 and 2016.

evidence.text: We generate most of our revenues from long-term U.S. Government contracts for design, production, and support activities. factors.summary: The company's pricing power appears to be limited, primarily due to the prevalence of fixed-price government contracts which constrain price adjustments. Additionally, revenue growth is driven more by volume increases rather than price changes, further indicating a lack of strong pricing power.

evidence.text: Our long-term contracts typically fall into one of two broad categories: Flexibly-Priced Contracts and Firm Fixed-Price Contracts. Approximately 92%, 94%, and 95% of our revenues for the years ended December 31, 2017, 2016, and 2015, respectively, were generated from flexibly-priced contracts, including certain fixed-price incentive contracts that have exceeded their cost-share limit.

Changes in sales and service revenues are typically expressed in terms of volume. Unless otherwise described, volume generally refers to increases (or decreases) in reported revenues due to varying production activity levels, delivery rates, or service levels on individual contracts. Volume changes will typically carry a corresponding income change based on the margin rate for a particular contract.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Limited pricing power due to fixed-price government contracts and reliance on volume growth.

factors.summary: The program execution is facing mixed challenges with some delays and cost overruns in specific programs, while overall execution appears to be on track. There are risks related to potential reductions in defense spending and budget uncertainties, which could lead to significant delays or cost overruns affecting profitability and financial stability. The company may also face material adverse effects on its financial position due to changes in customer priorities and potential contract terminations. Additionally, significant reductions in the number of ships procured or delays in funding could have a material impact on financial outcomes. Despite some programs experiencing execution challenges, others show improved performance, indicating a mixed execution landscape. However, the overall risk remains high due to external budgetary and spending uncertainties, which could exacerbate existing issues and lead to further delays, cost overruns, and technical problems in major programs. The company's ability to mitigate these challenges will be critical to maintaining profitability and operational stability.

evidence.text: "Significant reductions in the number of ships procured by the U.S. Navy or significant delays in funding our ship programs would have a material effect on our financial position, results of operations, or cash flows. The budget environment... may result in fewer contract awards and lower revenues, profits, and cash flows from our U.S. Government contracts."

"Significant delays or reductions in appropriations for our programs, changes in customer priorities, and potential contract terminations could have a material adverse effect on our financial position, results of operations, or cash flows."
"If our actual experience differs significantly from our assumptions or projections or we incur unanticipated contract costs, the profitability of the related contracts may be adversely affected."
evidence.subsection: Our earnings and profitability depend upon our ability to perform our contracts.

evidence.text: "Segment operating income in 2017 was $688 million, compared to $715 million in 2016. The decrease was primarily due to lower risk retirement on the delivered USS John P. Murtha (LPD 26), lower volume and risk retirement in the Virginia class (SSN 774) submarine program and Arleigh Burke class (DDG 51) destroyers, favorable changes in overhead costs in 2016, the receipt in 2016 of a local government incentive grant, and the establishment of an allowance for accounts receivable on a nuclear and environmental commercial contract in 2017, partially offset by higher risk retirement on Tripoli (LHA 7) and Portland (LPD 27), the resolution of outstanding contract changes on the inactivation of the decommissioned Enterprise (CVN 65) and the RCOH of the redelivered USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN 72), and improved performance in oil and gas services."

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Mixed execution with delays, cost overruns, and risks from budget uncertainties affecting profitability.
